w:r.w:t: System Architecture · Integration Blueprint · Safety Layer Design
Romania + EU Standards | Dual Mode: Architectural & Engineering | 100% Free & Open-Source Stack

This document defines the complete architecture for a professional-grade Construction AI 3D Viewport — a dual-mode (Architectural / Engineering) intelligent platform that integrates real structural physics, Romanian and EU building codes, multi-disciplinary AI expert agents, blueprint generation, and a dual-pass safety layer that cross-validates every calculation against independent solvers. All tooling is 100% free and open-source.
Zero-mistake policy — every calculation is run twice by independent engines and compared
Code-first — Romanian P100, Eurocodes EC0–EC9, Romanian zoning law baked into every decision
Multi-agent debate — specialist AI agents argue structural, architectural, MEP, fire, and energy positions
Full BIM parity — IFC4 native, AutoCAD layer standards, ISO 5457 title blocks
Geo-location aware — seismic zone, snow/wind loads, climate zone auto-resolved from coordinates
Blueprint-ready output — generates DXF/PDF/IFC from both modes at any LOD (100–500)
Every tool below is free, open-source, and carries no commercial licensing cost. Each is assigned a role within the system architecture.
Parametric 3D modelling, IFC authoring, walls/slabs/beams/columns with semantic data. Python-scriptable. github.com/FreeCAD/FreeCAD — LGPL
Full IFC authoring inside Blender. Native IFC4 read/write. github.com/IfcOpenShell/IfcOpenShell — GPL
3D rendering, photo-realistic visualisation, animation. Python API. blender.org — GPL
Programmatic solid modelling for precise parametric components. openscad.org — GPL
Read/write/modify IFC files, geometry kernel, clash detection, 2D drawing generation. Core BIM data bus of the entire system. LGPL
Browser-side IFC parsing and WebGL rendering via WASM. github.com/IFCjs/web-ifc — MIT
IFC validation, COBie exchange, model checking for .NET integration layer. github.com/xBimTeam — CDDL
Open BIM server: versioned model storage, multi-user collaboration, IFC diff/merge. github.com/opensourceBIM/BIMserver — AGPL
Primary structural FEA engine. Nonlinear static & dynamic (seismic) analysis, fiber sections, time-history. PEER/UC Berkeley — BSD. pip install openseespy
Secondary 2D frame & truss FEM solver. Used as INDEPENDENT cross-check engine in the Safety Layer. pip install anastruct — MIT
3D FEA for frames and trusses. Third validation pass for 3D cases. github.com/JWock82/PyNite — MIT
NREL port of Frame3DD. Wind/seismic frame analysis. BSD.
Matrix stiffness method, eigenvalue (modal) analysis, load combinations. scipy.org — BSD
Open steel section database (IPE, HEA, HEB, UB, UC, CHS, RHS). github.com/robbievanleeuwen/section-properties — MIT
DOE whole-building energy simulation. Heating/cooling loads, HVAC modelling. github.com/NREL/EnergyPlus — BSD
High-level API over EnergyPlus. Geometry, schedules, HVAC systems. pip install openstudio — BSD
Solar radiation, daylight, wind-rose, UTCI comfort. Grasshopper + Python. github.com/ladybug-tools — GPL
Validated daylight + electric lighting simulation. LBNL — custom open licence
CFD airflow and natural ventilation analysis. openfoam.org — GPL
Generate AutoCAD-compatible DXF files with correct layer standards, line types, title blocks. pip install ezdxf — MIT
PDF blueprint generation from vector drawings. MIT/BSD
Structural diagrams: BMD, SFD, deflection curves, load envelopes. BSD
2D plan/section SVG output for web viewport. MIT
Image compositing for rendered sheets. PIL licence
WebGL 3D scene: orbit, section cuts, layer toggling, material preview. MIT
IFC model loading directly into Three.js scene. MIT
Frontend shell: dual-mode UI, panel system, agent chat interface. MIT
Point cloud and geospatial terrain context (site positioning). Apache 2
Map base for geo-location, zoning overlay, site boundary. BSD/MIT
Expert AI agents: structural, architectural, MEP, fire safety, energy, smart home. Powers the multi-agent debate engine.
Agent orchestration, tool use, memory, RAG over standards database. MIT
Vector store for Romanian codes (P100, NP, GT, C, I series) and Eurocodes (EN 1990–1999). MIT/Apache 2
Embedding of building codes for semantic retrieval. Apache 2
[Mode Selector] ◄─── Geo-Location Engine (lat/lng → seismic zone, climate zone, zoning)
│ SAFETY LAYER │ ◄── INDEPENDENT cross-check (AnaStruct / PyNite / SciPy)
│ (Dual-Pass │ Compares EVERY result — flags divergence > tolerance

User enters address or drops pin. OpenLayers resolves lat/lng. Geo engine queries: seismic zone (P100-1/2013), snow load zone (CR 1-1-3), wind pressure zone (CR 1-1-4), climate zone (energy), municipal zoning (POT/CUT/H limits).

ARCHITECTURAL mode focuses on spatial layout, facades, circulation, furniture, finishes, lighting design, accessibility (HG 923/2010). ENGINEERING mode focuses on structural grid, load paths, FEA, section sizing, foundation design, MEP routing, energy compliance.

User sketches or imports geometry. FreeCAD/Bonsai handles parametric BIM authoring. IfcOpenShell assigns IFC4 types: IfcWall, IfcBeam, IfcColumn, IfcSlab, IfcDoor, etc. AutoCAD layer standard applied automatically (A-WALL, S-BEAM, E-POWER, P-WATER...).

Each specialist agent analyses the model and raises issues or proposals. E.g. Structural Agent flags column spacing > 6m without intermediate support. Architectural Agent objects to a load-bearing wall conflicting with open-plan intent. Debate Moderator arbitrates, proposes compromise (e.g. concealed steel beam). All agent reasoning is logged and shown in the UI.

OpenSeesPy runs FEA: load combinations per SR EN 1990 (ULS/SLS), seismic analysis per EC8/P100 (response spectrum or time-history), beam/column/slab section design per EC2/EC3. OpenStudio runs energy simulation. Results stored in calculation database.

INDEPENDENT engines re-run identical problem: AnaStruct for 2D frames, PyNite for 3D frames, SciPy direct stiffness for simple checks. Results compared numerically. Divergence > 2% triggers a RED FLAG — user is warned, calculation is blocked until resolved.

Automated rule engine checks every result against: P100 drift limits, EC2 reinforcement ratios, EC3 slenderness limits, energy U-values (Legea 372), fire resistance ratings (P118), accessibility (HG 923), zoning POT/CUT/height limits. Pass/Fail shown per item.

Blueprint DXF generated with ezdxf (correct AutoCAD layers, scales, title block). PDF generated with ReportLab (ISO A0–A4, SR ISO 5457 title block). IFC4 exported for use in Revit, Archicad, BIMserver. Calculation report PDF with full derivations, assumptions, code references.
Architectural mode is the design-intent layer. It governs spatial quality, aesthetics, programme, and regulatory compliance from a planning/zoning perspective.
Room areas, aspect ratios, daylight factors per SR EN 17037, natural ventilation paths, circulation widths
POT (lot coverage %), CUT (floor area ratio), height limits, setbacks per local PUG/PUZ
Solar shading analysis (Ladybug), U-value check (Legea 372/EPBD), window-to-wall ratio, aesthetic massing
Ramp grades (max 8%), corridor widths (min 1.20m), lift dimensions, accessible WC layouts per HG 923/2010
Parametric furniture library (ISO/EN standard dimensions), clearance zones, ergonomic compliance
Permeable surface ratio, green area (min 20% residential), parking count per local norms
Radiance-based daylight simulation, lux levels per SR EN 12464, emergency lighting routes per P118
Travel distances, staircase widths, smoke compartmentation per P118-99
Engineering mode is the technical-calculation layer. Every element is sized, verified, and cross-checked against Eurocodes and Romanian normatives.
Column grid optimisation, load path visualisation, redundancy check per EC0 reliability classes
Dead loads (self-weight from density), imposed loads (EN 1991-1-1), snow (CR 1-1-3), wind (CR 1-1-4), seismic (P100/EC8), load combinations ULS + SLS per EN 1990 Table A1.2
Beam/slab/column/foundation reinforcement design. Min cover, min/max rebar ratios, shear links, lap splices
Section classification, bending/shear/axial checks, buckling curves, connection design
OpenSeesPy response spectrum + pushover. Drift limits per P100 (δ ≤ 0.005h for brittle cladding, ≤ 0.0075h general)
Bearing capacity (EC7/NP 112), settlement calculation, pile design if required, ground investigation prompt
OpenStudio heating/cooling loads, duct sizing, ventilation rates per I5 normativ + EN 15251
Pipe sizing (SR EN 806), pressure zones, drainage slope, sewer capacity per NP 003
Cable sizing (SR HD 60364), short-circuit current, protection coordination, earthing system per NTE 007
EnergyPlus simulation → energy class (A–G) per Legea 372/2005
KNX/BACnet topology, sensor placement, automation logic layers
CCTV coverage analysis, access control zones, intruder detection per SR EN 50131
Sprinkler hydraulic calculation, fire pump sizing per SR EN 12845, smoke extraction per P118
5. AI AGENT SYSTEM — MULTI-EXPERT DEBATE ENGINE

The system runs multiple specialist AI agents simultaneously. Each agent has a defined knowledge domain, a set of hard constraints (building codes), and a 'personality' that reflects its discipline's typical priorities. When agents disagree, the Debate Moderator agent facilitates resolution and logs the reasoning.
Domain: EC2, EC3, EC8, P100. Priorities: safety, load path continuity, column sizing. Argues for: larger sections, more columns, conservative seismic detailing.
Domain: RLU zoning, SR EN 17037, HG 923. Priorities: open space, natural light, aesthetics. Argues for: fewer columns, open plans, larger windows.
Domain: I5, NP 003, SR HD 60364, EN 15251. Priorities: service routing, accessible shafts, adequate floor zones. Argues for: interstitial ceiling space, riser positions early in design.
Domain: P118-99, SR EN 13501, EN 1365. Priorities: compartmentation, egress routes. Argues for: fire walls, protected stairwells, sprinklers.
Domain: Legea 372/2005, EPBD, NZEB targets. Priorities: insulation, thermal bridges, MVHR. Argues for: triple glazing, thicker walls, heat recovery.
Domain: KNX, BACnet, IEEE 802.11, GDPR. Priorities: sensor integration, automation topology. Argues for: conduit routing for data, hub locations.
Domain: SR EN 50131, EN 50133, GDPR. Priorities: CCTV coverage, access control zoning. Argues for: blind-spot elimination, secure server room.
Meta-agent. Scores each argument against: code compliance weight, cost impact, constructability. Produces ranked resolution proposals with trade-off summary.
Any design decision that affects two or more agent domains triggers a debate session.
Each affected agent states its position and the specific code clause that supports it.
Agents raise objections to conflicting positions with quantified impact (cost/safety/performance delta).
Each agent proposes a compromise that satisfies its hard constraints while accommodating others.
Debate Moderator scores proposals. Hard code violations = disqualified. Remaining proposals ranked by multi-criteria score. Top proposal presented to user.
User may override the recommendation. System logs the override, notes the code implication, and requires explicit acknowledgement.
All agents retrieve from a vector database (ChromaDB) populated with chunked and embedded versions of the following source documents:
SR EN 1990 (EC0) — Basis of structural design
EPBD 2010/31/EU + 2024 recast — Energy performance of buildings

PRINCIPLE: No structural, energy, or compliance result is accepted until it has been independently re-calculated by a second engine. Divergence above tolerance automatically blocks output and escalates to the engineer. This mirrors the peer-checking obligation in real engineering practice (four-eyes principle).

The Safety Layer sits between the Calculation Engine and the Output Generator. It is architecturally isolated — it has read-only access to inputs and cannot be influenced by Pass 1 results before running its own calculation.
OpenSeesPy (structural FEA) + OpenStudio (energy) + IfcOpenShell-derived geometry quantities
AnaStruct (2D frames), PyNite (3D frames), SciPy direct stiffness method (simple cases), hand-formula checks for standard beam/column cases
Numerical diff of: reactions, member forces, moments, deflections, story drifts, energy loads, reinforcement areas
> 2% — FAIL RED (output blocked, engineer review required)
A licensed engineer may override a YELLOW flag with signature. RED flags cannot be overridden — the model must be corrected.
Every calculation, both passes, tolerance result, and any overrides are stored in immutable audit log (timestamped, hashed).
Column axial load and moment — kN, kN·m
Nodal displacements and rotations — mm, mrad
Storey drift ratios — Δ/h (compared against P100 limits)
Fundamental period T1 — seconds (modal analysis cross-check)
Reaction forces at all supports — kN, kN·m
Required steel reinforcement area As — cm² (EC2 design)
Foundation bearing pressure — kPa (vs. allowable from EC7)
Annual heating demand — kWh/m²/year (vs. Legea 372 class limits)
Peak heating load — kW (for boiler/heat pump sizing)
Peak cooling load — kW (for chiller/AC sizing)
U-values per element — W/m²K (vs. minimum requirements)
Thermal bridge linear transmittance Ψ — W/mK
Pipe diameter vs. flow rate (Darcy-Weisbach) — velocity 0.7–3.0 m/s
Cable cross-section vs. load current (IEC 60364 tables)
Voltage drop along cable run — ≤ 3% lighting, ≤ 5% power
Ventilation rate per occupancy zone — L/s/person
When both passes complete, the Safety Layer produces a Verification Certificate appended to every calculation report. It contains:
Table of all checked quantities with Pass 1 value, Pass 2 value, delta %, and status (GREEN/YELLOW/RED)
Summary of all code compliance items (pass/fail per clause)
List of any agent debate outcomes and engineer overrides
Digital hash of the model state at time of calculation
Recommended actions for any yellow/red flags
Statement of assumptions (unit weights, soil parameters, live load category)
ag = 0.35g | Tc = 1.6 s | Very high seismicity
Zone I: 1.0 kN/m² | Zone II: 1.5 kN/m² | Zone III: 2.0 kN/m² | Zone IV: 2.5 kN/m² (CR 1-1-3/2012)
Zone I: 0.4 kPa | Zone II: 0.5 kPa | Zone III: 0.6 kPa (CR 1-1-4/2012)
Residential: 2.0 kN/m² | Office: 3.0 kN/m² | Commercial: 5.0 kN/m² | Parking: 2.5 kN/m² (EN 1991-1-1)
C20/25 for structural elements. C25/30 recommended for columns. C30/37 for foundations in aggressive soil.
Interior: 25 mm | Exterior: 35 mm | Foundation (non-aggressive): 50 mm | Foundation (aggressive): 65 mm
Class I (hospitals, schools): 1.4 | Class II (residential >2 floors): 1.2 | Class III (industrial): 1.0 | Class IV (storage): 0.8
Minimum class C (≤150 kWh/m²/year). Recommended B. NZEB target from 2021: near-zero energy.
R1 detached: 35% | R2 semi-detached: 40% | R3 collective: 45% | M mixed: 60% | C commercial: 70%
Fire egress routes — Color 1 (red) — LW 0.35mm — DASHDOT
Plot boundaries, setbacks, zoning, parking, landscaping, utility connections. Z-ZONE + A-SITE layers.
All levels: walls, doors, windows, furniture (arch mode), column/beam grid (eng mode). Room areas, accessibility paths.
Cross-sections showing floor heights, storey heights, structural depths, ceiling zones, window sill/head levels.
All four facades: materials hatch, window schedules, floor level lines, overall height.
Beam/column grid, slab spans, reinforcement layout zones, foundation plan. S- layers only.
Beam/column/slab rebar drawings: bar sizes, spacings, covers, laps, hooks — per EC2.
Separate HVAC, electrical, plumbing plans. Duct/pipe sizes annotated. Shaft locations. Riser diagrams.
Compartmentation, egress routes, extinguisher/hydrant positions, sprinkler zones, smoke detectors.
Main distribution board, sub-boards, circuit schedule, earthing system, consumer unit layout.
EnergyPlus output: monthly loads chart, envelope summary, energy class certificate template.
Drawn by / Checked by / Approved by (with signature blocks)
Shear: V(x) = dM/dx, direct stiffness method for frames
EC3 buckling curves a/b/c/d based on section and fabrication
Fb = Sd(T1)·m·λ | Sd from P100 response spectrum
Behaviour factor q (DCH RC frames: q = 4.5αu/α1)
Limit: L/250 total, L/500 post-partition per EC2
T1 ≈ Ct·H^(3/4) — approximate per EC8 (Ct = 0.075 RC frame)
Ladybug provides I (irradiance) from EPW weather file
NPER = (QH + QC + QDHW + Qlight + Qaux) / Anet kWh/m²/year
v ≤ 3.0 m/s supply, v ≥ 0.7 m/s drainage (self-cleaning)
Demand units per EN 806-3: WC=2, shower=2, basin=0.5, bath=3
Total flow: Qtot = K·√ΣDU [l/s] K=0.5 residential
min 1% for DN75, min 0.5% for DN100, min 0.3% for DN150+
I = P/(√3·U·cosφ) three-phase | I = P/(U·cosφ) single-phase
Select cable from IEC 60364-5-52 current capacity tables
Set up React + Three.js + web-ifc 3D viewport
Integrate IfcOpenShell Python backend (FastAPI or Flask)
Implement geo-location engine: lat/lng → seismic zone, snow/wind, zoning
Build Romanian + Eurocode vector knowledge base (ChromaDB)
Implement basic AutoCAD layer DXF export via ezdxf
Deploy OpenSeesPy Pass 1 engine for simple 2D frames
Deploy AnaStruct Safety Layer Pass 2 engine
Build Safety Layer comparator with GREEN/YELLOW/RED logic
Implement Architectural Mode toolset (zoning, accessibility, daylight)
Implement Engineering Mode toolset (full load combinations, EC2/EC3 design)
Deploy all 8 AI agents with LangChain orchestration
Build Debate Engine with conflict resolution protocol
Integrate Ladybug Tools for solar/wind analysis
Implement blueprint generation: floor plans, sections, elevations
Full 3D seismic analysis: P100 response spectrum + pushover in OpenSeesPy
PyNite integration for 3D frame Safety Layer cross-check
MEP engineering: HVAC duct sizing, electrical single-line, plumbing risers
Fire safety engineering: sprinkler hydraulics, smoke extraction
Blender rendering pipeline for photo-realistic views
Engineer override workflow with digital signature
Performance optimisation: large model LOD management
Test against 5 real Romanian construction projects
